facts.claim: The distutils package has been removed from the standard library.
PEP 695 introduces a new, more compact and explicit syntax for declaring generic classes and functions.
PEP 701 lifts f-string restrictions, allowing any valid Python expression, including quote reuse, multi-line expressions, comments, and backslashes.
PEP 684 introduces a per-interpreter GIL, enabling sub-interpreters to utilize multiple CPU cores (currently C-API only).
Builtin hashlib implementations for SHA1, SHA3, SHA2-384, SHA2-512, and MD5 are replaced with formally verified code from the HACL* project for security.
PEP 709, comprehension inlining, speeds up dictionary, list, and set comprehensions by up to two times.
The asyncio package has received significant performance improvements, with some benchmarks showing a 75% speed up.
A new `type` statement is introduced for declaring type aliases, which can also be generic.
PEP 669 defines a new low-impact monitoring API for profilers and debuggers, supporting near-zero overhead.
PEP 688 makes the buffer protocol accessible from Python code, allowing classes with `__buffer__()` to be used as buffer types.
isinstance() checks against runtime-checkable protocols are 2 to 20 times faster.
Producing tokens via the tokenize module is up to 64% faster due to PEP 701 changes.
The `wstr` field is removed from Unicode objects in Python’s C API, reducing the size of every `str` object by at least 8 bytes.
Setuptools is no longer pre-installed in virtual environments created with `venv`, requiring explicit installation if needed.
The `asynchat`, `asyncore`, and `imp` modules have been removed from the standard library.
F-strings can now be arbitrarily nested due to lifted restrictions on expression components.
Stack overflow protection is implemented on supported platforms.
The `tarfile` and `shutil.unpack_archive()` functions have a new `filter` argument to limit dangerous features during extraction.
Support for the `perf` profiler is added via a new environment variable, command-line option, and `sys` functions.
Writing to sockets in `asyncio` has been significantly improved, avoiding unnecessary copying and using `sendmsg()`.
New `asyncio.eager_task_factory()` functions allow opting into eager task execution, speeding up some use-cases by 2x to 5x.
Improved 'Did you mean …' suggestions are provided for `NameError`, `ImportError`, and `SyntaxError` exceptions.
The `pathlib.Path` class now supports subclassing.
A command-line interface has been added to the `sqlite3` module.
The `typing.override()` decorator (PEP 698) is introduced.
Type aliases and type variable bounds/constraints declared via the new syntax are lazy-evaluated, allowing forward references.
Error messages for f-strings are now more precise, showing the exact location of the error.
Slice objects are now hashable, enabling their use as dictionary keys and set items.
sum() now uses Neumaier summation to improve accuracy and commutativity when summing floats or mixed ints and floats.
The `sys.last_exc` attribute is added for the last unhandled exception, deprecating `sys.last_type`, `sys.last_value`, and `sys.last_traceback`.
